_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-13R Amiodarone (Revised 12/10/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2026). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-13R: Amiodarone — MDHHS Approved 1/5/2026, Reviewed 2026
indications: Cardiac arrest — ventricular fibrillation (V-Fib) or pulseless ventricular tachycardia (pVT) refractory to defibrillation
Unstable tachycardia — irregular narrow complex tachycardia
Unstable tachycardia — regular wide complex tachycardia
contraindications: Severe sinus node dysfunction without pacemaker
Known hypersensitivity to amiodarone or iodine
precautions: Hypotension is a common side effect — monitor BP closely
Can prolong QT interval — risk of Torsades de Pointes in pre-existing QT prolongation
Dilute for slow infusion in stable tachycardia indications (150 mg over 10 minutes)
Bolus for cardiac arrest indications — do not slow push in pulseless arrest
expected_effects: Reduction in dysrhythmia recurrence in V-Fib/pVT
Slowing of ventricular rate in tachyarrhythmias
Conversion or rate control in ventricular tachycardia
notes: Cardiac arrest: give 300 mg IV/IO bolus; may repeat 150 mg once
Stable tachycardia: 150 mg slow IV over 10 minutes — do NOT bolus in perfusing rhythm
Pediatric arrest: 5 mg/kg IV/IO; max first dose 300 mg; may repeat twice; max total 450 mg
Suspected V-Tach with pulse: 150 mg over 10 minutes; max total 450 mg

dosing.dose: 300 mg IV/IO; may repeat 150 mg once
dosing.notes: Give after 3rd shock in refractory V-Fib/pVT

dosing.population: Adult (>14 yr) — unstable tachycardia (narrow irregular or wide regular with pulse)
dosing.notes: Do NOT bolus in perfusing rhythm; dilute in D5W when available

dosing.population: Adult (>14 yr) — suspected V-Tach with pulse
dosing.dose: 150 mg over 10 minutes; may repeat; max total 450 mg
dosing.notes: Monitor BP during infusion; stop if hypotension develops

dosing.dose: 5 mg/kg; max single dose 300 mg; may repeat twice; max total 450 mg
